name: Maddy Kelly
blurb: Vancouver-to-LA half-Irish, half-Indian sharpshooter; Tonight Show debut at 25; writes the Canadian sketches that go viral.
bio: Maddy Kelly is the new-wave Canadian comic everyone keeps clipping — half Irish, half Indian, raised in Vancouver, now based in LA. She did her first set four days after she turned the legal drinking age in B.C., made the 2023 Just For Laughs New Faces class, and broke through on a Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon set that opens on tickling-and-consent and lands on Hilary Duff worship. She's a staff writer on This Hour Has 22 Minutes — the Canadian sketch show that turned Trump's 'Quiet, piggy' remark into a viral Seuss parody — and the co-creator/host of CBC's Webby-winning podcast triptych Let's Make A Sci-Fi, Let's Make A Rom-Com, and Let's Make A Horror.

podcast.name: Let's Make A Sci-Fi / Rom-Com / Horror

timeline.ev: Born June 6 in Vancouver, British Columbia
First open-mic set in Vancouver, four days after turning 19
Let's Make A Sci-Fi launches on CBC; makes Vulture's Best Podcasts of 2022 list
Just For Laughs New Faces of Comedy (Montreal); joins This Hour Has 22 Minutes as staff writer
Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon stand-up debut
Wrote on the Quiet Piggy! Seussian-Trump sketch that became the most-shared 22 Minutes bit of the year

accolades: 2023 Just For Laughs New Faces of Comedy (Montreal)
Webby Award for Let's Make A Sci-Fi (CBC, co-host/writer)
Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon stand-up debut (2024)
Staff writer credit on This Hour Has 22 Minutes (CBC)

movies_tv.t: The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon

ranking_notes: Maddy Kelly is the new-wave Canadian fast-riser worth tracking — Tonight Show + JFL New Faces in the same beat means she's on the canonical short-list for the next ranking pass.
